title: Portfolio CMS
longDescription:
Overview

Portfolio CMS is a bespoke Content Management System (CMS) designed to bridge the gap between static site generation and dynamic content management. Built to run entirely within the Google Workspace ecosystem, it transforms a simple Google Sheet into a powerful database and backend for a professional, React-based portfolio website. This project eliminates the need for traditional hosting fees, complex database setups, or third-party CMS subscriptions, offering a truly serverless and zero-cost solution for creatives.

Key Features

  • Spreadsheet-as-a-Database: Utilizes Google Sheets as a relational database to store project metadata, categorized tags, gallery links, and global site settings.
  • Visual Admin Interface: A custom-built, responsive web application (deployed via Google Apps Script) serves as the admin panel. It features:
  • Rich Text Editing: Integrated Quill.js editors for formatting project descriptions with bold, italics, lists, and headers.
  • Dynamic List Management: Drag-and-drop style interfaces for managing gallery images and downloadable files.
  • Live Preview & Publishing: One-click publishing that generates a static HTML file, injects the latest data, and saves it directly to Google Drive for hosting via GitHub Pages or similar platforms.
  • Smart Automation:
  • Featured Project Logic: Automatically handles "Featured" status across categories, ensuring only one project per category is highlighted on the hero board.
  • Media Handling: Automatically detects YouTube links to generate thumbnails and embedded players, while standardizing image URLs.
  • Data Sanitization: Cleans and formats incoming data to ensure consistent front-end rendering.
  • Modern Front-End: The generated website is a single-file React application styled with Tailwind CSS, featuring:
  • Glassmorphism Design: A modern, dark-mode aesthetic with blurred backdrops and smooth transitions.
  • Responsive Layouts: Fully adaptive grids for mobile, tablet, and desktop views.
  • Interactive Components: Custom-built lightboxes, modals, and navigation menus.

Technical Stack

  • Backend: Google Apps Script (Serverless Node.js environment)
  • Database: Google Sheets API
  • Frontend (Admin): HTML5, Tailwind CSS, Vanilla JavaScript
  • Frontend (Public Site): React 18, Tailwind CSS, Lucide React Icons
  • Integrations: EmailJS (Serverless contact form), Google Analytics 4

The "Why"

As a multidisciplinary designer, I needed a way to update my portfolio without touching code every time I finished a project. Existing solutions were either too rigid (Wix/Squarespace) or too complex/expensive for a simple static site (Contentful/Sanity). Portfolio CMS was born from the desire to own the entire stack—data, logic, and presentation—while keeping the maintenance overhead near zero.

title: LithoLab
description: LithoLab Web is a browser-based application designed to transform standard 2D images into fully 3D-printable, full-color CMYK lithophanes. Unlike traditional lithophane generators that primarily support monochrome depth mapping, this application processes images into distinct color and texture layers tailored for multi-filament 3D printing. It uniquely integrates the Google Gemini API to allow users to generate base photos and high-contrast vector-style masks on the fly directly within the creative environment.
longDescription:
LithoLab Web is a browser-based application designed to transform standard 2D images into fully 3D-printable, full-color CMYK lithophanes. Unlike traditional lithophane generators that primarily support monochrome depth mapping, this application processes images into distinct color and texture layers tailored for multi-filament 3D printing. It uniquely integrates the Google Gemini API to allow users to generate base photos and high-contrast vector-style masks on the fly directly within the creative environment.


The user workflow centers around a highly interactive canvas where source images and generated masks are positioned, scaled, and rotated. Once the visual layout is finalized, the application moves into the processing phase, mathematically mapping the pixel data into CMYK and white channels to determine exact geometric depth. Finally, the tool exports a comprehensive ZIP archive containing individual STL files for each filament color alongside precise instructions for printing and filament swapping.


Under the hood, the application is engineered with TypeScript and Vite, leveraging native HTML5 Canvas APIs for performant, pixel-level image manipulation. The heavy lifting of 3D mesh generation is handled entirely client-side using custom algorithms and the `@jscad/modeling` library, ensuring that complex mathematical conversions from 2D pixels to 3D facets happen efficiently without relying on an external rendering backend.

title: Unfold
description: Unfold is a high-fidelity reflection platform that transforms prompt-based journaling into an interactive, digital experience. Unlike static journaling applications, Unfold utilizes a dynamic card-shuffling engine and a sophisticated deck-based architecture to provide structured introspection. Its primary value lies in its ability to balance pre-curated psychological prompts with a robust user-defined content system, making it adaptable for both therapeutic use and creative professional workflows.
longDescription:
Unfold is a high-fidelity reflection platform that transforms prompt-based journaling into an interactive, digital experience. Unlike static journaling applications, Unfold utilizes a dynamic card-shuffling engine and a sophisticated deck-based architecture to provide structured introspection. Its primary value lies in its ability to balance pre-curated psychological prompts with a robust user-defined content system, making it adaptable for both therapeutic use and creative professional workflows.


The application is organized into three functional modules: the Journey Setup for session parameterization, the immersive Card Display for active reflection, and a History Panel for auditing past insights. Users navigate from an initial configuration phase through a focused session where they can flip cards, mark favorites, and track their progress through a deck. This workflow ensures that data moves seamlessly from high-level session parameters to granular, individual prompt interactions and eventual long-term archival.


Built on a modern React and TypeScript foundation, Unfold prioritizes performance and data privacy. The technical core features a custom-built state management layer using React Hooks and the LocalStorage API, ensuring all sensitive user reflections remain strictly client-side. The UI is powered by Tailwind CSS and Radix UI primitives, providing an accessible, responsive, and aesthetically refined interface that maintains smooth transitions during complex deck interactions.

title: GeoLens Pro
description: GeoLens Pro is a mobile-first Progressive Web Application (PWA) designed for field professionals who need to capture, organize, and annotate geo-tagged photos. The app solves the common problem of managing large volumes of field photos by automatically sorting them based on GPS location into predefined job zones.
longDescription:
GeoLens Pro - Portfolio Breakdown

Overview

GeoLens Pro is a mobile-first Progressive Web Application (PWA) designed for field professionals who need to capture, organize, and annotate geo-tagged photos. The app solves the common problem of managing large volumes of field photos by automatically sorting them based on GPS location into predefined job zones.


The Problem It Solves

Field workers (surveyors, inspectors, contractors, real estate professionals) often capture hundreds of photos across multiple job sites. Manually organizing these photos is:

  • Time-consuming - Hours spent sorting and renaming files
  • Error-prone - Photos easily get assigned to wrong projects
  • Disconnected - No link between photos, locations, and client data


The Solution

GeoLens Pro uses GPS geofencing to automatically sort photos into jobs the moment they're captured. If you're standing at a job site and take a photo, the app knows exactly which job it belongs to - no manual sorting required.


Core Features

1. GPS-Powered Auto-Sort Engine

The heart of the application. When a photo is captured or imported:

  1. GPS coordinates are extracted (live from device or from EXIF metadata)
  2. The Haversine formula calculates the distance to each active job's origin
  3. If the photo falls within a job's geofence radius, it's automatically assigned
  4. If multiple jobs overlap, the closest one wins
  5. Photos outside all zones go to "Unassigned" for manual sorting
// Haversine formula implementation for GPS distance
const R = 6371000; // Earth's radius in meters
const distance = R * c; // Distance between photo and job origin
if (distance <= job.radius) → Assign to job


2. Job Management with Geofencing

  • Define job locations using an interactive Leaflet map
  • Set custom geofence radii (feet, meters, miles, or kilometers)
  • Visual radius preview on the map
  • Jobs can be Active or Archived
  • Smart sorting options: Recent Activity, A-Z, Newest, Oldest


3. Client CRM

  • Full contact management (company, contact person, email, phone, address)
  • Link clients to jobs for organized project tracking
  • Alphabetically sorted client list
  • Swipe-to-delete with confirmation


4. Camera Integration

  • Live camera mode with real-time GPS indicator
  • Front/rear camera toggle
  • Flash control
  • Photo import with EXIF metadata extraction
  • High-resolution capture (up to 4032x3024)


5. Advanced Photo Editor

  • Multi-layer transparency system - Create annotation layers like slides
  • Drawing tools - Brush and eraser with customizable colors and sizes
  • Image transforms - Rotate (90 degree increments), flip horizontal/vertical
  • Cropping - With coordinate system that maintains drawing positions
  • Per-layer transforms - Each layer can have its own crop/rotation
  • Undo/redo history
  • Layer management (add, delete, rename, reorder)


6. Slideshow Viewer

  • Fullscreen presentation mode
  • Swipe/tap navigation through all layers
  • Title and caption display
  • Navigation indicators


7. PDF Export

  • Professional document generation with jsPDF
  • Header with job title, client info, and location
  • 2x2 grid layout of all layers (Original + annotations)
  • Captions preserved under each image
  • Perfect for reports, documentation, or printing

title: PixelForge AI
description: PixelForge AI is a serverless, client-side generative art studio that leverages Google's Gemini and Imagen models. It features a robust local database for gallery management, intelligent prompt augmentation, and a "Bring Your Own Key" architecture that prioritizes user privacy and ease of deployment.
longDescription:
PixelForge AI is a dedicated workspace application designed to streamline the generation and curation of AI artwork. Unlike standard chat interfaces, this application provides a highly structured environment optimized specifically for image synthesis, exposing advanced granular controls like aspect ratios, negative prompts, and resolution settings. Its primary differentiator is the direct, flexible integration with Google's Imagen 4.0 API, supporting dual-endpoint routing to handle various generation parameters and safety filters natively.


The user journey is centered around a unified workspace module where creators construct and refine prompts using integrated templating tools. Once a generation is triggered, the asynchronous process is managed via global state, allowing users to continue working without interruption. Completed images flow directly into a comprehensive history dashboard, where users can inspect generation metadata, view high-resolution details, and export their assets in batch using built-in client-side ZIP compression capabilities.


Under the hood, the application is powered by a React 18 frontend written in TypeScript and styled with Tailwind CSS for a responsive, dark-mode-first aesthetic. Complex asynchronous API interactions and cross-component data synchronization are orchestrated seamlessly through Zustand, ensuring a highly performant and stable user experience even when handling large base64 image payloads.

title: Chronicle: Iterative Fiction Engine
description: Chronicle is a multi-modal interactive fiction engine designed to generate dynamic, choice-driven narratives in real time. Unlike traditional text adventures with pre-written paths, this application leverages generative AI to dynamically construct the story, illustrations, and voice narration based on continuous user decisions. Operating completely without a backend server, it securely orchestrates complex, multi-step API workflows directly from the client browser.
longDescription:
Chronicle is a multi-modal interactive fiction engine designed to generate dynamic, choice-driven narratives in real time. Unlike traditional text adventures with pre-written paths, this application leverages generative AI to dynamically construct the story, illustrations, and voice narration based on continuous user decisions. Operating completely without a backend server, it securely orchestrates complex, multi-step API workflows directly from the client browser.


The journey begins in a configuration interface where players define their desired genre, visual style, and initial premise. The application then transitions into a primary story terminal displaying narrative text, synthesized scene imagery, and audio playback controls. Throughout the experience, the system silently extracts lore to populate a living codex of entities and artifacts, while a built-in state manager allows users to pause, resume, or export their completed adventure as a formatted document.


Under the hood, the application is powered by a zero-infrastructure React architecture running within a single HTML file. It integrates the Google Gemini API for complex narrative generation and text-to-speech, paired with the Google Imagen API for stylized image synthesis, demonstrating advanced prompt engineering, resilient fallback handling, and robust client-side data persistence.

title: Strata - Digital Notebook
description: A modern, block-based note-taking web application with Google Drive integration and an infinite canvas workspace.
longDescription:
Overview

Strata is a full-featured note-taking app built with React, offering hierarchical organization, cloud sync, and a flexible canvas for visual notes. It runs entirely in the browser with no backend server, using Google Drive for cloud storage and localStorage as an offline fallback.


Key Features

Organizational Structure

  • Hierarchical organization: Notebooks → Tabs → Pages
  • Color-coded tabs with automatic cycling
  • Favorites system for quick access
  • Responsive tab navigation with fish-eye hover effects


Rich Text Editing

  • Block-based editor supporting headings, lists, todos, images, videos, links, and dividers
  • Slash commands (/) for quick block insertion
  • Drag-and-drop block reordering
  • Real-time formatting with keyboard shortcuts


Canvas Pages

  • Infinite canvas with pan and zoom (Alt+scroll wheel)
  • Freeform drawing with customizable brush colors and widths
  • Text containers for annotations
  • Image pasting and manipulation
  • Transform state persistence per page
  • Live drawing feedback


Cloud Integration

  • Google Drive sync with automatic debounced saves
  • Embed Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides directly in pages
  • PDF viewing from URLs or Google Drive
  • Portable backup system with offline viewer
  • Two-way sync with conflict resolution


User Experience

  • Full dark mode support
  • System preference detection for theme
  • Session-wide page caching for fast navigation
  • Offline support with localStorage fallback
  • No backend required — runs entirely client-side


Technical Highlights

  • Built with React (JSX) and transpiled in-browser with Babel
  • Google Drive API integration for cloud storage
  • Coordinate system handling for infinite canvas with viewport culling
  • Real-time drawing with SVG path rendering
  • Responsive design with Tailwind CSS
  • Zero-dependency architecture (except Google APIs)

title: IdeaForge.AI
description: IdeaForge AI is an ideation and execution workspace designed to eliminate the friction between conceptualizing a product and building it. It replaces fragmented note-taking tools by providing a continuous pipeline for raw idea capture, interactive brainstorming, and campaign management.
longDescription:
IdeaForge AI is a personal, AI-powered workspace designed to capture and structure raw creative thoughts. Unlike standard note-taking tools that rely on proprietary cloud backends, this application ensures total data sovereignty by utilizing the user's personal Google Drive and Google Sheets as a headless, relational database.


The application guides users through a structured creative lifecycle. The journey begins in the Ideas module, where users capture stream-of-consciousness text or voice notes. These raw inputs are processed by AI into organized concepts, which can then be escalated through dedicated Brainstorming workspaces, structured Project task boards, and final marketing Campaign trackers. 


Under the hood, the system is built on a modern React and TypeScript frontend, heavily utilizing TanStack Query for state management and data synchronization. Its primary technical achievement is the complex integration of the Google Sheets REST API to perform robust database operations—including filtering, row migrations, and schema updates—entirely client-side.

title: CharGen.AI
description: CharGen AI is a comprehensive design suite that automates the creation of multi-dimensional character profiles and visual assets. Unlike standard random generators, this application enforces strict logical consistency across psychological, physical, and demographic traits. It utilizes structured JSON generation via Google's Gemini API to ensure every attribute contextually aligns with the character's core identity.
longDescription:
CharGen AI is a comprehensive design suite that automates the creation of multi-dimensional character profiles and visual assets. Unlike standard random generators, this application enforces strict logical consistency across psychological, physical, and demographic traits. It utilizes structured JSON generation via Google's Gemini API to ensure every attribute contextually aligns with the character's core identity.


The application features a modular workflow guiding users from initial ideation to asset export. Users begin in the trait manipulation forms to fine-tune attributes like MBTI, physical build, and origin. This data feeds into the Generation Panel, where prompt engines synthesize visual representations—ranging from profile portraits to technical T-pose reference sheets—and author narrative backstories tailored to specific genres.


Built on a React and Vite frontend, the application utilizes Zustand for complex state management across its creation modules. The primary technical achievement is the custom prompt translation layer, which dynamically parses dozens of UI inputs into optimized, high-fidelity instructions for seamless integration with multimodal AI endpoints.

title: LithoPalletGen
description: A browser-based palette generator for color lithophane 3D printing. Upload reference images, sample colors with an area-averaging eyedropper, and export structured JSON palettes compatible with PIXEstl.
longDescription:
LithoPalletGen is a specialized color calibration tool designed for multi-material lithophane workflows. Unlike simple color pickers, this application employs an area-averaging eyedropper that samples every pixel within a configurable region (32 to 256px), producing statistically accurate color values that account for print texture, lighting variance, and filament translucency across photographed swatch samples.


The application features a split-pane workspace pairing a canvas-based image viewer with a hierarchical palette builder. Users upload photographs of their printed filament swatches, then systematically build multi-layer color profiles—each base color containing ordered layers representing increasing material thickness. The eyedropper overlays a live-updating sampling box on the image, averaging hundreds of pixels per capture to eliminate noise from surface inconsistencies inherent in FDM prints.


Built as a zero-dependency vanilla JavaScript application with no build step, the entire state architecture relies on IndexedDB for persistent image storage and localStorage for palette serialization. The primary technical achievement is the real-time pixel averaging engine, which performs per-frame getImageData computations across thousands of pixels while maintaining responsive cursor tracking, and a dual-format export system that generates PIXEstl-compatible JSON in either HSL or hex notation from a single unified color model.
